title: Day 15 Quiz — Context, this, and Theme Architecture
description: Verify understanding of this/call/apply/bind, Context API typing, theme provider patterns, persistence, and global state trade-offs.

questions.prompt: Arrow functions have their own this binding that refers to the enclosing lexical scope.
questions.explanation: Arrow functions do NOT have their own this — they inherit this from the enclosing scope. Regular functions get this from how they are called.

options.text: The result of calling the function immediately
A new function with this permanently set to the provided value
A copy of the arguments array
undefined

questions.explanation: bind returns a new function with this fixed. It does not invoke the original function.

questions.prompt: What is the main difference between call() and apply()?

options.text: call() is for classes; apply() is for functions
call() accepts arguments individually; apply() accepts an array of arguments
apply() binds this permanently; call() does not
There is no difference

questions.explanation: Both invoke immediately with an explicit this. call(fn, arg1, arg2) vs apply(fn, [arg1, arg2]).

questions.prompt: Where should ThemeProvider be placed in a React app?

options.text: Inside every page component that needs theme
At the app root so all descendants can consume theme context
Only inside the theme toggle component
Below the router so routes do not re-render

questions.explanation: Global providers belong at the root — above routes and layout — so every component can access context without duplicate providers.

questions.prompt: Which cause unnecessary Context consumer re-renders? Select all that apply.

options.text: Passing a new object literal as context value every render
Memoizing context value with useMemo and stable dependencies
Including a new function reference in context value without useCallback
Using a primitive string as the only context value

questions.explanation: New object/function references each render change context value identity, re-rendering all consumers. Primitives and memoized values are stable.

questions.prompt: Consume context in functional components with the {{blank1}} hook. Create context with React.{{blank2}}.
questions.explanation: createContext defines the context object; useContext reads the nearest Provider value.
fn() is called without a receiver — in non-strict sloppy mode this is global/window; in strict mode undefined. The method lost its this binding when extracted.

questions.prompt: When should you NOT use React Context?

options.text: For theme preference shared across the entire app
For state that only two nested components need, passable via props
For authentication session available to many routes
For locale/i18n shared globally

questions.explanation: React docs recommend props for shallow sharing. Context is for truly global or widely consumed state — not a replacement for all prop passing.
Function types document setter signatures. Dispatch<SetStateAction<T>> is valid when backed by useState.

questions.prompt: How do you persist theme to localStorage without causing a flash of the wrong theme on page load?
questions.sampleAnswer: Read localStorage synchronously in the useState initializer function so the first render uses the stored preference. For zero flash, some apps inject a tiny inline script in index.html before React hydrates to set data-theme on html.
questions.explanation: Reading in useEffect runs after first paint — causing flash. Synchronous read on init is the minimum fix.
